items.message: Thank you for your feedback. Tagging and routing to the team member(s) best able to assist.

items.body:
## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage

**Summary:** The `Azure.Storage.Blobs` client is requesting an OAuth token scope with a double slash (`(storage.azure.com/redacted) instead of the expected single slash (`(storage.azure.com/redacted), which breaks custom token caching layers.

<details>
<summary>📋 Issue Details</summary>

**Package:** `Azure.Storage.Blobs 12.27.0`
**Affected API:** `BlobContainerClient.ExistsAsync` / `TokenCredential` scope construction
**Scenarios:**
- Implementing a custom `TokenCredential` and observing that `TokenRequestContext.Scopes` contains `(storage.azure.com/redacted) (double slash)
- Token caching layers that do exact-match or normalized comparison on scopes fail to cache correctly

**Root ask:** Fix the scope string passed in `TokenRequestContext` so it uses a single slash (`(storage.azure.com/redacted) rather than a double slash.
</details>

<details>
<summary>🔎 Debugging / Reproduction Notes</summary>

The double slash suggests the resource URI (`(storage.azure.com/redacted) is being concatenated with `.default` directly, with the trailing slash on the resource URI not being stripped before appending `/.default`. This is likely in the scope construction logic within the Storage client internals.

**Suggested investigation steps:**
1. Search for `storage.azure.com` in `sdk/storage/` to find where the resource URI and scope are assembled (e.g., `StorageConnectionString`, `StorageSharedKeyCredentialPolicy`, or authentication helper classes).
2. Verify whether the trailing slash on the resource base URL (`(storage.azure.com/redacted) is being preserved when `.default` is appended — look for patterns like `$"{resourceUri}/.default"` where `resourceUri` already ends in `/`.
3. Confirm the fix normalizes the scope to `(storage.azure.com/redacted) and add a unit test asserting the correct scope value is passed to `TokenCredential.GetToken`.
</details>

<details>
<summary>🏷️ Label Confidence</summary>

- **Category:** `Client` — `Azure.Storage.Blobs` is a data-plane client library (not a management/ResourceManager package and not a Provisioning package).
- **Service:** `Storage` — Explicitly named in the issue title and body; confirmed by the `sdk/storage/` directory.
- **Confidence:** High — Package name is explicitly stated, service and category are unambiguous, and matches quality reference issues (e.g., #50084, #49299) which use `Client` + `Storage`.
</details>

<details>
<summary>👥 Owner Routing</summary>

- **Matched CODEOWNERS entry:** `# ServiceLabel: %Storage` (line 493) — only `%Storage` is required and the issue has that label.
- **AzureSdkOwners:** none listed
- **ServiceOwners:** `@amnguye` `@jalauzon-msft` `@jaschrep-msft` `@nickliu-msft` `@seanmcc-msft`
- **Routing action:** Added `Service Attention` and `needs-team-attention` labels; issue left unassigned per ServiceOwners-only path; owners mentioned via routing comment.
- **Scan notes:** Scanned bottom-to-top; no `%Storage %Mgmt` or other multi-label Storage entries were encountered. The single-label `%Storage` entry at line 493 was the first (and only) match. The `%Synapse`, `%Tables`, and management-specific entries above and below did not match.
</details>
